title: Conjecture 1.4 (Ordered Binary Matrix Removal Lemma)
statement_text: For any finite family $\mathcal{F}$ of ordered binary matrices and any $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $\delta = \delta(\mathcal{F}, \varepsilon)$ such that any $n \times n$ binary matrix which is $\varepsilon$-far from $\mathcal{F}$-freeness contains at least $\delta n^{a+b}$ copies of some $a \times b$ matrix from $\mathcal{F}$.
context_text: Alon, Fischer and Newman obtained an efficient conditional regularity lemma for ordered binary matrices and conjectured that it could be used to yield a removal lemma for ordered binary matrices. This paper proves Theorem 1.8, an infinite ordered matrix removal lemma that settles a generalized form of this conjecture, extending it to finite or infinite families over any finite alphabet.
paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.8 (Infinite Ordered Matrix Removal Lemma), which settles a generalized form of this conjecture for any finite or infinite family of ordered matrices over any finite alphabet $\Sigma$.
paper_title: Testing hereditary properties of ordered graphs and matrices

title: Open Problem: Regularity-free proof for the ordered removal lemma
statement_text: It will be interesting to try to obtain a proof for the ordered graph removal lemma that does not go through the strong regularity lemma needed in the current proof.
context_text: The proofs of the ordered removal lemmas rely on strong variants of the graph regularity lemma, which impose a wowzer-type (tower-of-towers) dependence between the parameters $\delta^{-1}$ and $\varepsilon^{-1}$. Fox gave the first regularity-free proof of the unordered graph removal lemma, though still with tower-type dependence; an analogous approach for the ordered setting remains open.
notes: Stated as a research direction in Section 1.3 without a formal labelled environment.
paper_title: Testing hereditary properties of ordered graphs and matrices

title: Open Problem: Better parameter dependence for ordered binary matrix removal
statement_text: It will be interesting to combine the ideas from the current proof with the binary matrix regularity lemma of Alon, Fischer and Newman to obtain a removal lemma for finite families of ordered binary matrices with better dependence between the parameters $\delta^{-1}$ and $\varepsilon^{-1}$; ideally polynomial dependence, but even exponential dependence would be interesting.
context_text: For ordered binary matrices, Alon, Fischer and Newman obtained an efficient conditional regularity lemma in which $\delta^{-1}$ is polynomial in $\varepsilon^{-1}$. The general ordered removal lemma proved in this paper inherits a wowzer-type dependence from the strong regularity lemma, and improving this for finite families of ordered binary matrices is posed as an open problem.
notes: Stated as a research direction in Section 1.3 without a formal labelled environment.
paper_title: Testing hereditary properties of ordered graphs and matrices
